questions.question: Where should we store transcript files on KVM - in the database as text content (like artifacts) or as files on disk with static file serving?

options.label: Database (inline content)
options.description: Store transcript content as TEXT in database. Simple, no file I/O, automatic versioning. Good for transcripts under ~1MB.

options.label: Filesystem (disk storage)
options.description: Store as files in /opt/stack/AIStudio/storage/transcripts/. Better for large files, requires Express static serving setup.

questions.question: When should transcripts be copied from laptop to KVM?

options.label: Immediately on completion
options.description: Copy transcript when agent completes (record_agent_complete). Proactive, transcripts always available, but uses more storage.

options.label: On-demand viewing
options.description: Copy only when user clicks 'View Transcript'. Saves storage, but requires user to wait for transfer.

questions.question: How should transcripts be displayed in the UI?

options.label: Raw JSONL format
options.description: Show the raw transcript file as-is. Simple, preserves all data, but less user-friendly.

options.label: Parsed and formatted
options.description: Parse JSONL and display conversation turns, tool calls, and responses in a readable format. Better UX, requires parsing logic.

questions.question: What size transcripts do you expect? This affects storage and transfer approach.

options.label: Small (<100KB)
options.description: Typical short sessions. Database storage is fine.

options.label: Medium (100KB-1MB)
options.description: Most sessions. Either approach works.

options.label: Large (1MB-10MB+)
options.description: Long sessions or many tool calls. Filesystem storage recommended.

questions.question: Should the daemon run as a separate process or integrate with the existing remote agent (laptop-agent)?

options.label: Separate daemon process
options.description: New standalone service that watches transcripts. Simpler, dedicated purpose, but adds another process to manage.

options.label: Integrate with remote agent
options.description: Add 'watch-transcripts' capability to existing laptop-agent. Reuses infrastructure, single process, but more complex.

questions.question: How should the daemon match new transcripts to workflow runs?

options.label: Parse transcript content
options.description: Read first few lines of new transcript to extract sessionId/agentId, then match to active workflows. More reliable but requires file I/O.

options.label: Watch running-workflows.json
options.description: Monitor .claude/running-workflows.json for session registrations, match by filename. Faster but relies on hook tracking.

options.label: Hybrid approach
options.description: Watch running-workflows.json first, fall back to parsing transcript content if no match found. Best of both worlds.

questions.question: When should the daemon call add_transcript?

options.label: Immediately on file creation
options.description: Register as soon as new .jsonl file appears. Fastest, but might register non-agent files.

options.label: After first write (debounced)
options.description: Wait for file to have content (200ms debounce). Ensures file is valid before registering.

options.label: After validating content
options.description: Read first line to confirm it's a valid transcript. Most reliable but slowest.

questions.question: How should next_step behave: always return current step (requiring explicit 'advance' call), or have a parameter to mark previous complete and advance?

options.label: Auto-advance
options.description: next_step always advances to next step (assumes previous is done)

options.label: Explicit parameter
options.description: markCurrentComplete parameter controls whether to advance

options.label: Separate tools
options.description: get_current_step (read-only) + advance_step (moves forward)

questions.question: Should these tools work independently from the existing Docker-based Story Runner, or integrate with it?

options.label: Independent
options.description: Any LLM can orchestrate directly via MCP, no Docker runner needed

options.label: Integrated
options.description: Works alongside Docker runner, shares state via checkpoints

options.label: Replace runner
options.description: New tools replace the Docker runner entirely

questions.question: How should approval gates be handled when encountered?

options.label: Return prompt
options.description: Return instructions telling orchestrator to call respond_to_approval

options.label: Block until resolved
options.description: Tool waits/polls until approval is given, then returns next step

options.label: Skip/flag
options.description: Flag in response that approval is needed, orchestrator decides

questions.question: Which parts of this plan should we prioritize for the first implementation?

options.label: All together
options.description: Implement all 4 solutions as one cohesive change

options.label: Step commands first
options.description: Focus on get_current_step/advance_step/repeat_step only

options.label: Story key first
options.description: Add story key resolution to existing tools first (quick win)

options.label: Session context first
options.description: Add set_context to eliminate repeated projectId

questions.question: What type of implementation do you want for ST-190?

options.label: Execute Live Test
options.description: Run a workflow execution now to manually verify transcript registration works (I'll execute the story with a team and verify transcripts)

options.label: Write Automated E2E Tests
options.description: Create Playwright E2E tests that can be run repeatedly to verify transcript registration

options.label: Both
options.description: Execute a live test now AND create automated E2E tests for future regression testing

questions.question: Which team should be used for testing?

options.label: Simplified Dev Workflow
options.description: 3 states (exploration → implementation → testing) - faster execution, good for quick verification

options.label: Standard Dev Workflow
options.description: 9 states - more comprehensive but longer execution time

options.label: Create Minimal Test Workflow
options.description: Create a new minimal 1-state workflow specifically designed for transcript testing

questions.question: Where should these workflow control buttons appear?

options.label: WorkflowExecutionMonitor only
options.description: Add buttons to the existing workflow monitor page (/workflow-runs/:runId)

options.label: StoryDetailPage only
options.description: Add buttons to the story detail page, visible when viewing a story

options.label: Both pages
options.description: Add buttons to both pages for maximum accessibility

questions.question: For 'Mark phase to skip' - what should this do?

options.label: Skip future phases
options.description: Mark a not-yet-executed state to be skipped when the runner reaches it

options.label: Skip current and continue
options.description: Skip the currently running/paused phase and move to the next state

options.label: Both options
options.description: Provide UI to skip current phase OR mark future phases to skip

questions.question: Should the 'Run workflow' button replace or complement the existing story execution path?

options.label: New control panel
options.description: Add a dedicated 'Workflow Controls' panel with all 4 buttons grouped together

options.label: Header actions
options.description: Add buttons to the page header alongside existing Refresh/Back buttons

options.label: Inline with states
options.description: Add control buttons contextually next to workflow states

questions.question: After implementing Skott integration, how should we handle existing files in the database that have no dependency data?

options.label: Run full project analysis
options.description: Queue a full analyze-project job to populate all files with dependency data (~few minutes)

options.label: Lazy populate on access
options.description: Only populate when get_file_dependencies is called for a specific file

options.label: No backfill needed
options.description: Just let new commits/analyses populate data going forward

questions.question: Which workflow should I use for ST-196?

options.label: Standard with Approved Plan
options.description: 6 states - Security → Architecture → Approval → TDD → Implementation → Review (recommended since plan exists)

options.label: Simplified Dev Workflow
options.description: 3 states - Quick exploration → implementation → testing

options.label: Skip workflow, implement directly
options.description: No workflow tracking - just implement the plan now

questions.question: Do you approve proceeding with the implementation of ST-196 (Skott integration)?

options.label: Approve
options.description: Proceed with implementation as planned

options.label: Approve with changes
options.description: Proceed but I have specific modifications

options.label: Reject
options.description: Do not proceed - need more analysis

questions.question: How should the Master CLI session receive MCP commands? The story suggests injecting text prompts like 'Call record_agent_start with...' but there's an alternative approach using Claude Code's native MCP bridge. Which approach do you prefer?

options.label: Text Prompt Injection
options.description: Runner sends natural language prompts to Claude CLI asking it to call MCP tools. Claude parses and executes. More flexible but requires result parsing.

options.label: Direct MCP via CLI stdin
options.description: Use Claude Code's --mcp-server flag to register VibeStudio MCP, then send tool calls directly. More reliable but requires CLI MCP support verification.

questions.question: Should we keep the AgentSession class for backward compatibility or fully remove it in favor of Task tool spawning?

options.label: Remove AgentSession
options.description: Fully commit to Task tool approach. Simpler architecture but no fallback.

options.label: Keep as Fallback
options.description: Keep AgentSession for edge cases where Task tool doesn't work (e.g., specific tool restrictions).

questions.question: Based on my explanation, which MCP execution approach should we use for ST-200?

options.label: Text Prompt Injection
options.description: Send natural language prompts asking Claude to call MCP tools. Aligned with story description and existing pre/post instruction flow.

options.label: Direct MCP Protocol
options.description: Use Claude Code's native MCP protocol for structured tool calls. More deterministic but requires verifying CLI support.

questions.question: Should we update ST-200 to use your existing laptop agent infrastructure (spawn_agent MCP) instead of Task tool spawning?

options.label: Use Laptop Agent
options.description: Master session calls spawn_agent MCP → work dispatched to your laptop via WebSocket. Uses existing infrastructure.

options.label: Keep Task Tool
options.description: Keep original plan with Task tool. Would require installing Claude Code in Docker container on KVM.

questions.question: The core profile includes ~30 most-used tools. Should I adjust this list based on your workflow, or is the usage-based selection good?

options.label: Usage-based is good
options.description: Use the top 30 tools from transcript analysis

options.label: I'll review the list
options.description: Show me the full list, I'll suggest changes

options.label: Add specific tools
options.description: I have specific tools that must be in core

questions.question: Should we also trim verbose tool descriptions as part of this work? Currently descriptions are 500-1500 chars each.

options.label: Yes, trim them
options.description: Reduce descriptions to 1-2 sentences (~50% more savings)

options.label: No, keep verbose
options.description: Descriptions help Claude understand tool usage

options.label: Separate story
options.description: Do it later as a separate optimization

questions.question: For the Playwright MCP server (~14k tokens), how should we handle it?

options.label: Keep always loaded
options.description: I use browser testing frequently

options.label: Disable by default
options.description: Only enable when I need browser testing

options.label: Out of scope
options.description: Don't change Playwright config

questions.question: Review the 28-tool core profile. Any tools to add or remove?

options.label: Looks good
options.description: Proceed with this list

options.label: Add tools
options.description: I need specific tools always available

options.label: Remove tools
options.description: Some tools should be invoke_tool only

questions.question: For the ~125 non-core tools, should I optimize all of them now, or focus only on the core 28 + confusion clusters and defer the rest?

options.label: Core 28 + clusters only
options.description: Optimize 28 core tools + ~20 confusion cluster tools (~50 total). Faster, immediate impact.

options.label: All 153 tools
options.description: Complete optimization of every tool. More work but consistent codebase.

questions.question: Should artifacts persist and accumulate across workflow runs of the same story, or should each new run replace the previous artifacts?

options.label: Accumulate
options.description: New runs add new versions, all history preserved. E.g., ARCH_DOC v1 from run 1, v2 from run 2, etc.

options.label: Replace (Latest)
options.description: Each run overwrites artifacts. Only the most recent version exists per story.

options.label: Inherit + Extend
options.description: New runs start with previous run's artifacts and can modify/add to them.

questions.question: What's the primary use case driving this change?

options.label: Cross-run continuity
options.description: Agents in new runs need to see/use artifacts from previous failed/partial runs.

options.label: Story-level reporting
options.description: Want to view all artifacts for a story regardless of which run produced them.

options.label: Reduce duplication
options.description: Same artifacts get regenerated on every run, wasting time and tokens.

options.label: Artifact evolution
options.description: Track how artifacts (like arch docs) evolve across multiple runs of the same story.

questions.question: When planning without a story context (e.g., general exploration), where should the plan artifact go?

options.label: Require story first
options.description: User must set story context before planning. Error if no story.

options.label: Project-level artifacts
options.description: Create project-level artifacts for plans without story context. Extend artifact system.

options.label: Keep .md fallback
options.description: Use artifacts when story exists, fall back to .md files when no story context.

questions.question: Which workflow should we use for ST-214 (Story-Scoped Artifacts)?

options.label: With Approved Plan (Recommended)
options.description: Skips exploration/planning since plan is approved. Goes: Security → Architecture → Approval → TDD → Implementation → Review

options.label: Standard Dev Workflow
options.description: Full 9 states including exploration and planning phases. More thorough but redundant given existing plan.

options.label: Simplified Workflow
options.description: Only 3 states (explore → implement → test). Fast but may miss security/architecture review for this complex change.

questions.question: Do you approve proceeding to TDD Testing and Implementation with the security fixes incorporated?

options.label: Approve
options.description: Proceed with implementation. Security fixes will be incorporated during TDD and implementation phases.

options.label: Request Changes
options.description: Need modifications to the plan before proceeding.

options.label: Cancel Workflow
options.description: Stop the workflow and revisit the requirements.

questions.question: Should the automatic tracking be always-on, or controlled by a flag (e.g., `autoTrackAgent: boolean` in advance_step params)?

options.label: Always automatic
options.description: Simplest approach - always track agent start/complete when transitioning to/from agent phase

options.label: Optional flag
options.description: Add autoTrackAgent param to advance_step to enable/disable automatic tracking (default: true)

questions.question: What should happen if record_agent_start or record_agent_complete fails during advance_step?

options.label: Fail the transition
options.description: Return error from advance_step if tracking fails - ensures consistent state

options.label: Log and continue
options.description: Log warning but continue with phase transition - tracking is non-blocking

options.label: Return warning
options.description: Complete transition but include warning in response about tracking failure

questions.question: How should we handle the componentSummary field that record_agent_complete expects?

options.label: Optional param
options.description: Add componentSummary as optional param to advance_step when transitioning from agent phase

options.label: Auto-generate
options.description: Generate a basic summary from the output data automatically

options.label: Skip it
options.description: Don't require componentSummary for automatic tracking - it can be added manually later

questions.question: The code review found 4 critical issues. How would you like to proceed?

options.label: Fix issues first
options.description: Go back to implementation to fix the migration SQL and types.ts issues before deployment.

options.label: Skip to verification
options.description: Proceed with TypeScript check and deployment as-is (may fail if issues exist).

options.label: Complete workflow
options.description: Mark workflow as complete with code review findings documented. Fix issues in a follow-up story.

questions.question: ST-214 is a backend/schema change. How should we handle the Playwright verification phase?

options.label: Skip & Complete
options.description: Skip Playwright verification and mark workflow complete. Deploy will happen separately.

options.label: Run verification anyway
options.description: Deploy to production and run Playwright to verify no regressions.

options.label: Mark as pending
options.description: Pause workflow here. Complete verification later after manual deployment.

questions.question: For the structured componentSummary format (status, summary, keyOutputs, nextAgentHints, artifactsProduced), should it be stored as JSON or as formatted text?

options.label: JSON object
options.description: Structured JSON in componentSummary field - easier for programmatic access and display flexibility

options.label: Formatted text
options.description: Markdown/text with standard sections - simpler, human-readable in raw form

questions.question: Should we remove the dead `outputSummary` field from ComponentRun as part of this work?

options.label: Yes, remove it
options.description: Clean up dead code - outputSummary exists in schema but is never used or displayed

options.label: No, keep separate
options.description: Keep outputSummary for potential future use or backwards compatibility

questions.question: Where should the unified summary be displayed? Select all that apply.

options.label: Workflow Results panel
options.description: Current WorkflowResultsSummary side panel showing per-state summaries

options.label: Story detail page
options.description: Show agent summaries on story detail page alongside implementation summary

options.label: Workflow list views
options.description: Show brief summary in workflow run list/cards

options.label: All of the above
options.description: Unified display everywhere summaries are shown

questions.question: Which tests are hanging - backend unit tests (npm test), backend E2E tests, or frontend tests?

options.label: Production

options.label: Docker Runner (primary)

questions.question: How should the taxonomy be managed?

options.label: Static per-project (Recommended)
options.description: Each project has a fixed list of areas. Simple, predictable, opt-in (empty = no validation)

options.label: Dynamic with auto-learning
options.description: System learns new areas as used, auto-suggests normalization. More complex.

questions.question: When a use case is created with area 'Login' (a synonym), what should happen?

options.label: Strict rejection with suggestions (Recommended)
options.description: Error: 'Login' not in taxonomy. Did you mean 'Authentication'? Explicit control.

options.label: Auto-normalize with warning
options.description: Warning: 'Login' normalized to 'Authentication'. Stores canonical name.

options.label: Auto-normalize silently
options.description: Just store canonical name, no warning. Simplest but less transparent.

questions.question: How should existing use cases with inconsistent areas be handled?

options.label: Report-only mode (Recommended)
options.description: Add get_taxonomy_violations tool to identify issues. Safe, non-breaking.

options.label: Lazy normalization
options.description: Keep existing areas, validate only new/updated. Background job suggests fixes.

options.label: Migration script
options.description: One-time script to normalize all existing areas. Requires manual review.

questions.question: What level of detail should the taxonomy include?

options.label: Flat areas only (Recommended)
options.description: Simple: { areas: [...], synonyms: {...} }. Easy to implement and use.

options.label: Hierarchical categories
options.description: Nested: { categories: { 'User Mgmt': { areas: [...] } } }. More complex.

questions.question: When a use case is created with a NEW area 'Mobile App' not in taxonomy, what should happen?

options.label: Smart suggestion (Recommended)
options.description: If similar to existing area: reject with suggestion. If genuinely new: auto-add to taxonomy.

options.label: Always auto-learn
options.description: Auto-add any new area to taxonomy. Simple but may accumulate variations.

options.label: Always reject
options.description: Reject all unknown areas. User must manually add via manage_project_taxonomy first.

questions.question: For the master plan artifact at epic level - do you want this to be a special ArtifactDefinition that all epics can use, or defined per-workflow?

options.label: Global definition
options.description: Single 'THE_PLAN' definition available to all epics across all workflows

options.label: Per-workflow
options.description: Each workflow defines its own epic-level artifact types (more flexible but more setup)

questions.question: Should the ArtifactWatcher auto-detect and upload artifacts when you write them during Claude discussions (before workflow), or only during workflow execution?

options.label: Always watch
options.description: Any file written to docs/EP-XXX/ or docs/ST-XXX/ gets uploaded immediately

options.label: Workflow-only
options.description: Only upload artifacts when a workflow run is active

options.label: Manual trigger
options.description: Add a command/tool to explicitly sync local docs to database

questions.question: When writing docs/EP-XXX/THE_PLAN.md during a Claude discussion (no active workflow), how should we identify which PROJECT the artifact belongs to?

options.label: Use current project
options.description: Use the project from MCP context (set_context or .vibestudio config). Assumes single project per workspace.

options.label: Parse from EP key
options.description: Lookup epic by key (EP-XXX) and derive project. Fails if epic doesn't exist yet.

options.label: Require epic first
options.description: Epic must exist in DB before artifacts can be uploaded. You'd create epic, then write THE_PLAN.

questions.question: What's your expected workflow - create epic in VibeStudio first, then write the plan, or write the plan file first and have epic created automatically?

options.label: Epic first
options.description: Create epic in VibeStudio UI/API, then write docs/EP-XXX/THE_PLAN.md

options.label: File first
options.description: Write docs/EP-XXX/THE_PLAN.md and have system auto-create the epic if it doesn't exist

questions.question: Does this plan look good to proceed, or do you have changes/concerns?

options.label: Looks good, proceed
options.description: Exit plan mode and create stories in VibeStudio

options.label: Needs changes
options.description: I have feedback on the plan before proceeding

questions.question: Should Native Planner also be able to UPDATE THE_PLAN since it helps you during plan mode, or should it remain read-only like other agents?

options.label: UPDATE (like Explorer/Architect)
options.description: Native Planner can modify THE_PLAN since it's part of the planning phase

options.label: READ-ONLY (Recommended)
options.description: Native Planner only consumes THE_PLAN - you create/update it yourself in plan mode

questions.question: How should agents know the epic context? Currently stories have epicId but agents don't automatically receive it.

options.label: Add epic info to story context
options.description: Orchestrator includes epicId and epicKey in the prompt when spawning agents

options.label: Agents check via MCP
options.description: Agents use get_story to retrieve epicId, then check for docs/EP-XXX/THE_PLAN.md

options.label: Skip epic support for now
options.description: Implement story-level only, add epic support later

questions.question: How aggressive should the refactoring budget be for the Developer agent?

options.label: 20% (Recommended)
options.description: Spend up to 20% of implementation time on opportunistic refactoring. Balanced approach.

options.label: 10% (Conservative)
options.description: Minimal refactoring, focus on primary implementation. Slower debt reduction.

options.label: 30% (Aggressive)
options.description: More time on cleanup, faster debt reduction but longer story completion times.

questions.question: The cleanup scope is small - only 1 unused file to delete. Do you also want me to test the transcript streaming on the live page to verify everything works?

options.label: Delete + Verify
options.description: Delete ArtifactViewer.tsx and manually verify transcript streaming works on the live page

options.label: Delete Only
options.description: Just delete the unused ArtifactViewer.tsx file

questions.question: How should Tauri frontend communicate with the Node.js sidecar?

options.label: HTTP health endpoint (Recommended)
options.description: Use existing port 3002 health endpoint. Simpler, already implemented in ST-334. Just poll /health for status.

options.label: JSON-RPC over stdio
options.description: Communication via stdin/stdout as mentioned in ST-342. More complex but allows richer bidirectional messaging.

options.label: Both - HTTP now, JSON-RPC later
options.description: Start with HTTP for ST-335 (quick win), add JSON-RPC in ST-342 for advanced features.

questions.question: Does the Gemini CLI actually exist and what's its output format? The plan assumes --output-format=json-stream but I couldn't find documentation confirming Gemini CLI has this.

options.label: Research needed
options.description: We need to research the actual Gemini CLI capabilities first

options.label: Assume similar to Claude
options.description: Proceed assuming Gemini CLI has similar streaming JSON output

options.label: Skip for now
options.description: Create the interface abstraction first, add Gemini when we know more

questions.question: The existing IAgentExecutor interface is tightly coupled to ClaudeCodeJobPayload. Should we create a generic AgentJobPayload that both Claude and Gemini use?

options.label: Generic AgentJobPayload
options.description: Create a new generic type, adapters convert to/from CLI-specific payloads

options.label: Keep ClaudeCodeJobPayload
options.description: Gemini executor also takes ClaudeCodeJobPayload (simpler, less flexible)

options.label: Per-executor payloads
options.description: Each executor has its own payload type, interface uses a union type

questions.question: How should we handle the agent:claude_job vs agent:gemini_job routing? The plan suggests separate events, but this creates backend coupling.

options.label: Single agent:job event
options.description: Single event with executorType field, agent routes internally (Recommended)

options.label: Separate events
options.description: Keep agent:claude_job and agent:gemini_job as proposed in the plan

options.label: Capability-based
options.description: Server checks agent capabilities and sends appropriate event type

questions.question: What's the priority: Should we complete ST-G1 (interface abstraction) now since it's already 90% done, or wait to plan all stories together?

options.label: Complete ST-G1 now
options.description: Finish the interface abstraction since most work is done

options.label: Plan all first
options.description: Complete the full architecture review before any implementation

options.label: Create all stories
options.description: Create ST-G1 through ST-G5 in the system, then prioritize
